concept.conceptName: Fibrin D-dimer | Platelet poor plasma | Coagulation
Fibrin D-dimer [Presence] in Platelet poor plasma
Fibrin D-dimer [Presence] in Platelet poor plasma by Latex agglutination
Fibrin D-dimer [Titer] in Platelet poor plasma

en.name: Fibrin D-dimer in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Fibrin D-dimer is a degradation product of cross-linked fibrin, formed by the sequential action of three enzymes — thrombin, factor XIIIa, and plasmin — and is a specific marker of fibrin formation and fibrinolysis [1]. The main clinical use is the exclusion of venous thromboembolism (VTE): a normal D-dimer in a patient with a low or intermediate clinical pretest probability has a high negative predictive value and rules out VTE without imaging [1, 2]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by an immunoassay on platelet-poor plasma or, for some point-of-care devices, whole blood. **Note on units — D-dimer units (DDU) vs fibrinogen-equivalent units (FEU).** D-dimer can be reported in two distinct unit systems — **DDU** (mass of the D-dimer fragment) and **FEU** (mass expressed as the parent fibrinogen molecule) — and **the two are not numerically equivalent**. Conversion between systems is assay-dependent and not standardised across manufacturers [3]. The same patient sample run on a DDU-reporting assay vs. an FEU-reporting assay will yield different numerical values. **Always preserve the unit (DDU or FEU) when mapping** — never strip or convert silently. The LOINC code itself encodes which unit system is used (suffix `.DDU` vs `.FEU` in the LOINC long name). ## Included Concepts The set is built around two LOINC Hierarchy concepts, each expanded to include all their descendants. Together they pull in nine LOINC "Lab Test" concepts covering quantitative D-dimer measurements in platelet poor plasma (PPP) and in whole blood. **Platelet poor plasma**, anchored on *37072890 / LP394010-5 — Fibrin D-dimer | Platelet poor plasma | Coagulation*: - *3051714 / 48065-7 — Fibrin D-dimer FEU [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma*: most widely used FEU code worldwide; default for FEU sources that do not specify the assay method. - *3052648 / 48067-3 — Fibrin D-dimer [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay.FEU*: same quantity as `48065-7`, with the immunoassay method explicit. - *3048530 / 48066-5 — Fibrin D-dimer DDU [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma*: default for DDU sources that do not specify the assay method. - *3052716 / 48058-2 — Fibrin D-dimer [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay.DDU*: same quantity as `48066-5`, with the immunoassay method explicit. **Whole blood** (point-of-care assays), anchored on *1470777 / LP443381-1 — Fibrin D-dimer | Blood | Coagulation*: - *42870366 / 71427-9 — Fibrin D-dimer [Mass/volume] in Blood by Rapid immunoassay*: most common LOINC code for whole-blood point-of-care D-dimer devices. - *37021210 / 91556-1 — Fibrin D-dimer [Mass/volume] in Blood by Immunoassay.DDU*: whole-blood D-dimer reported in DDU. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: - DDU codes: **`ug/L{DDU}`** or equivalently `ng/mL{DDU}`. - FEU codes: **`ng{FEU}/mL`** or `mg{FEU}/L`. Source systems may report `mg/L`, `ug/mL`, `mg/L FEU`, `ng/mL DDU`, etc. — the unit string and the LOINC code together unambiguously specify the result. Conversion between DDU and FEU is assay-dependent [3]; do not assume a fixed factor without consulting the assay package insert. ## Excluded Concepts The two hierarchy anchors above also pull in three qualitative LOINC concepts that are explicitly excluded: - *3015823 / 15179-5 — Fibrin D-dimer [Presence] in Platelet poor plasma*: qualitative result (present / absent), not a quantitative measurement. Cannot be merged with numerical mg/L values. - *3033464 / 29280-5 — Fibrin D-dimer [Presence] in Platelet poor plasma by Latex agglutination*: same as above, with a method specification. - *3038383 / 38898-3 — Fibrin D-dimer [Titer] in Platelet poor plasma*: titer (e.g. 1:8, 1:16) is a dilution-based result of historical assays — different unit system, different statistical handling. The set also deliberately omits the following LOINC concepts that exist for D-dimer: - **Cerebrospinal fluid** specimens (e.g. `40777694 / LP52466-7 — Fibrin D-dimer | Cerebral spinal fluid`): out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Panel concepts** (e.g. `37032186 / LP394459-4 — Short Fibrin D-dimer FEU and DDU panel`): grouping concepts, not measurement results. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "D-dimer", "DDi", "DD", "D-dimères" without further specification, on platelet-poor plasma, in **mg/L FEU** or **ng/mL FEU** (the most common reporting convention worldwide): `3051714 / 48065-7 — Fibrin D-dimer FEU [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma`. - **Source reports DDU**: map to `3048530 / 48066-5 — Fibrin D-dimer DDU [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma` (or `3052716 / 48058-2` if the source explicitly mentions immunoassay method). - **Source uses a whole-blood point-of-care assay** (Cardiac Reader, Triage, etc.): map to `42870366 / 71427-9 — Fibrin D-dimer [Mass/volume] in Blood by Rapid immunoassay`. - **DDU vs FEU disambiguation**: when the source unit string is ambiguous (e.g. just `mg/L` without DDU/FEU annotation), check the assay manufacturer documentation. - **Do not convert units silently**: if a source reports DDU and the recommended unit in the data dictionary is FEU (or vice-versa), preserve the original value and unit; conversion is assay-dependent. - **Do not confuse with**: fibrinogen degradation products (FDP — broader pool of fibrin/fibrinogen breakdown products, different LOINC concepts), soluble fibrin monomer (different marker), or thrombin-antithrombin complex. ## References [1] Adam SS, Key NS, Greenberg CS. D-dimer antigen: current concepts and future prospects. *Blood*. 2009;113(13):2878-87. Available: https://doi.org/10.1182/blood-2008-06-165845 [2] Konstantinides SV, Meyer G, Becattini C, et al. 2019 ESC Guidelines for the diagnosis and management of acute pulmonary embolism. *Eur Heart J*. 2020;41(4):543-603. Available: https://doi.org/10.1093/eurheartj/ehz405 [3] Longstaff C, Adcock D, Olson JD, et al. Harmonisation of D-dimer — A call for action. *Thromb Res*. 2016;137:219-220. Available: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.thromres.2015.11.031
